name: RawData+opencog-ml+chat-utils.scm not loading automatically
content:
Source: opencog-dev mailing list
Thread: chat-utils.scm not loading automatically
Date range: Mon, 22 Feb 2016 21:24:20 -0800 (PST) to Mon, 29 Feb 2016 18:12:01 +0800
Messages: 13
Participants: Tareq Alkhaldi, Linas Vepstas, Man Hin Leung, Leung Man Hin
---

From: Tareq Alkhaldi

Hi everyone,

So I had the setup working before, I haven't touched it in a while, but 
when I pulled the new code from repos and built, it doesn't seem to 
recognize nlp-parse (not loading chat-utils.scm) even though it loads [...]

---

From: Linas Vepstas
Date: Tue, 23 Feb 2016 14:52:11 -0600

The only "supported" way of using these files is as described in the README
files.  If you do anything different, you are on your own. This code is too
raw to do anything else.

For the two chatbots, that means starting them as `guile -l run-chatbot.scm`

In general, you don't want to load files by hand, instead, you want to
instead use modules.  Since nlp-parse is defined in the chatbot module, you
should (use-modules (opencog nlp chatbot))

Note that I am actively hacking chatbot-eva, so it will be highly unstable
in the coming weeks/months.  I believe that chatbot itself will probably be
mostly-stable, but it's hard to say.

--linas

p.s. it's probably wiser to start the cogserver inside of guile, rather
than starting guile inside the cogserver.   Just do this:  `(use-module
(opencog [...]

---

From: Linas Vepstas
Date: Tue, 23 Feb 2016 14:53:45 -0600

I mean, you can load files by hand, during development, all you want; just
that this would not be the normal way to script things in production.

--linas

On Tue, Feb 23, 2016 at 2:52 PM, Linas Vepstas <linasvepstas@gmail.com>
wrote:

"WordeNode" and "WordeInstanceLink"

tareq@tareq-ThinkPad-T430:/usr/local/src/opencog/build$ sudo guile -l [...]

---

From: Linas Vepstas
Date: Wed, 24 Feb 2016 12:56:44 -0600

The stuff in .guile is convenient but not necessary.

You should NOT use sudo to run guile!   The sudo command runs things as
root; this is generally a dangerous thing to do.  For example, if you use
sudo a lot, you'll probably now have a bunch of files owned by root, which
then interfere with future builds, because non-root users can't
delete/modify those files.  It turns into a death-spiral of weird bugs.

Both WordNode and WordInstanceNode are defined in (use-modules (opencog
atom-types)) so maybe something is failing to load this.   If I look
at /usr/local/share/opencog/scm/opencog/cogserver.scm it is loaded, so I
don't understand the cause of that error.

If I look at /usr/local/share/opencog/scm/opencog/nlp/fuzzy.scm its not
loaded, so that's a bug, I'll fix that right now.

Oddly, [...]

---

From: Linas Vepstas

It shouldn't.  I get this:

 Enter `,help' for help.
scheme@(guile-user)> (process-query "luser" "Who is John Kennedy?")
Hello luser, you said: "Who is John Kennedy?"
You made an Interrogative SpeechAct
$1 = "Sorry, I don't know the answer."
scheme@(guile-user)> (process-query "luser" "What is a phonograph?")
Hello luser, you said: "What is a phonograph?"
You made an Interrogative SpeechAct
$2 = "Sorry, I don't know the answer."
scheme@(guile-user)>

Nil is working on a major upgrade of the forward chainer, and may have
something to say about backward chaining.  I guess it works kind-of-ish but
might not be fast or efficient?  (??)

Before you start worrying about that, you might try hooking up [...]

---

From: Linas Vepstas
Date: Thu, 25 Feb 2016 11:37:41 -0600

On Thu, Feb 25, 2016 at 11:25 AM, Linas Vepstas <linasvepstas@gmail.com>
wrote:

Well, what I just wrote is not quite right: You do have to do some very
simple chaining:  "who questions can only take "person" as an answer",
together with "John Kennedy is a person".    However, it would probably be
best to just create a hack for this kind chaining at first, just to get
something, anything working.   It can then be replaced by some sort of
fancier chaining later on.

Likewise: "What is X?" questions can be answered only by "X is a thing", "X
is an object", "X is an inanimate object"  ... When is X? ... "X is a
date"... "When did X? "X is an event"

It would be great if there was enough plumbing in place that it could
answer these four types of questions, even with extremely simple [...]
